scope: EthicBit/CEMU v4.0 — evidence chain, KMS layer, CI/CD pipeline, Triple Public Anchor, Claim Boundary Engine, external validation interface, multi-agent runtime (AEM-EVOLVE)
mitigations_note: All mitigations reference current implemented controls. Gaps marked RESIDUAL_RISK.

stride_threats.threat: Attacker impersonates the KMS signer to produce fraudulent in-toto or SBOM signatures.
stride_threats.mitigation: KMS key alias/ethicbit-intoto-signing is IAM-controlled; signing requires explicit AWS credentials. Signer ARN embedded in every statement — verifiable against KMS DescribeKey.
stride_threats.residual_risk: Compromised AWS credentials would allow impersonation. Mitigated by IAM least-privilege; not fully eliminated.

stride_threats.threat: Attacker produces a fake external validator attestation to elevate claim status without genuine review.
stride_threats.mitigation: PROHIBITED_AUTOMATED_ELEVATIONS list blocks automated status elevation. human_attestation_status transitions require out-of-band human sign-off. CBE enforces external_claim_review_completed=false until explicitly overridden.
stride_threats.residual_risk: LOW — automated path is structurally blocked.

stride_threats.threat: Attacker injects a malicious GitHub Actions workflow that mimics slsa-build.yml or slsa_hybrid_attest.yml to produce fraudulent attestations.
stride_threats.mitigation: Workflows pinned to specific action versions. Builder ID in provenance.json references the canonical workflow URI. id-token: write permission scoped.
stride_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — supply chain compromise of GitHub Actions marketplace actions remains a residual risk.

stride_threats.threat: Attacker modifies a signed in-toto statement file after KMS signing to alter evidence content.
stride_threats.mitigation: Each statement contains payloadSHA256 of the canonical payload. Signature covers the SHA256 digest via ECDSA_SHA_256. Modification invalidates signature on verification.
stride_threats.residual_risk: LOW — cryptographic binding makes undetected tampering computationally infeasible.

stride_threats.threat: Attacker modifies claim_boundary_regression_cases.json to remove a blocking case, allowing an overclaim to pass.
stride_threats.mitigation: cases_sha256 and cases_canonical_sha256 recorded in every report. Hypothesis property test I-M verifies that any injected non-BLOCKED case flips report to FAIL_CLOSED. CI runs on every push.
stride_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — depends on CI not being bypassed.

stride_threats.threat: Attacker replaces anchor receipt files with fabricated TXIDs pointing to attacker-controlled transactions.
stride_threats.mitigation: Anchor TXIDs are publicly verifiable on Sepolia, Arweave, and AO. anchor_pre_deploy_gate.sh re-verifies live against public networks before any deploy. Anchor receipts also in git history.
stride_threats.residual_risk: LOW — public network verification makes fabrication detectable.

stride_threats.threat: Compromised dependency in npm or pip supply chain introduces malicious code into the hermetic build.
stride_threats.mitigation: npm ci uses package-lock.json (integrity hashes). SBOM covers 654 components with digests. slsa-build.yml uses --ignore-scripts on npm install.
stride_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — SBOM is signed but not continuously re-verified against registries.

stride_threats.threat: Signer denies having signed an in-toto statement or SBOM.
stride_threats.mitigation: KMS CloudTrail logs every Sign API call with timestamp, caller identity, and key ID. signedAt field embedded in each artifact. Triple Public Anchor timestamps the full evidence bundle.
stride_threats.residual_risk: LOW — KMS CloudTrail provides non-repudiable audit log.

stride_threats.threat: Agent output disputed; no traceable record links the output to the agent run parameters.
stride_threats.mitigation: CEERV formally freezes case_003 output as PASS_REFERENCE_ONLY. official_operational_status.json is canonical truth source. AEM-EVOLVE produces structured evidence bundles per execution.
stride_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — runtime evidence chain completeness depends on execution context.

stride_threats.threat: Live AWS infrastructure identifiers (KMS key IDs, RDS endpoints, EC2 instance IDs) published in public-facing state blocks, enabling targeted attacks.
stride_threats.mitigation: Sanitization policy enforced: all public state blocks use 'sanitized identifier' language. .gitignore excludes .env and credential files. KMS key IDs referenced only by alias in public artifacts.

stride_threats.threat: Evidence bundles contain sensitive operational data (execution parameters, internal system state) exposed via public mirror.
stride_threats.mitigation: Public mirror (EthicBit_CEMU_Public_Verification) receives same commits as origin. Sensitive data should be excluded from committed artifacts; .gitignore covers keys/, .env*.
stride_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — evidence artifacts may contain operational context that was not designed as public.

stride_threats.threat: AWS KMS becomes unavailable, blocking all signing operations and preventing release.
stride_threats.mitigation: KMS has SLA 99.99%. Signing is not on the critical execution path (done offline before anchor). Signed artifacts persist locally and in git.
stride_threats.residual_risk: LOW — signing is a pre-release step, not runtime dependency.

stride_threats.threat: Sepolia, Arweave, or AO networks become unavailable, blocking anchor verification and pre-deploy gate.
stride_threats.mitigation: anchor_pre_deploy_gate.sh blocks on verification failure. Receipts cached locally. Multiple RPC endpoints configured for Ethereum.
stride_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — gate failure blocks deployment; recovers when network resumes.

stride_threats.threat: Automated process attempts to elevate human_attestation_status or external_claim_review_completed without out-of-band human authorization.
stride_threats.mitigation: PROHIBITED_AUTOMATED_ELEVATIONS structural list. CBE enforces external_claim_review_completed=false. Hypothesis I-M meta-test blocks any automated path that returns PASS after injection.

stride_threats.threat: CI workflow escalates permissions beyond contents: read / id-token: write to push force or modify protected branches.
stride_threats.mitigation: GitHub Actions permissions declared minimally per workflow. No push permissions granted to CI workflows. Branch protection on main.
stride_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — depends on GitHub branch protection configuration being correctly enforced.

linddun_threats.threat: Evidence artifacts linking multiple agent executions to the same individual or organization enable profiling.
linddun_threats.mitigation: Evidence artifacts describe system behavior, not user behavior. Case IDs are abstract (case_003). No PII in committed artifacts.

linddun_threats.threat: Public anchor transactions on Sepolia/Arweave reveal deployment timing and operational cadence to adversaries.
linddun_threats.mitigation: Anchor receipts are intentionally public for tamper-evidence. Timing disclosure is an accepted trade-off.

linddun_threats.threat: Downstream users of AEM-EVOLVE outputs are unaware that the system is operating under HUMAN_ATTESTATION_PENDING status.
linddun_threats.mitigation: All public-facing materials declare current state machine position. Outreach pack explicitly states PENDING_EXTERNAL for criteria 1, 2, 8. CBE blocks external certification claims.
linddun_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — depends on reviewers reading the state disclosures.

linddun_threats.threat: AI system outputs used in regulated domains (finance, clinical, legal) without declared scope limitation.
linddun_threats.mitigation: CBE blocks financial_advice_claim, clinical_diagnostic_claim, regulatory_approval_claim. Non-claims section in all public documents. LINGO_AND_CLAIM_DICTIONARY defines scope.

linddun_threats.threat: Outreach pack or validator invitation reveals internal system details (architecture, key IDs, endpoint URLs) beyond what is necessary for validation.
linddun_threats.mitigation: Outreach materials reviewed for sanitization. Infrastructure IDs replaced with 'sanitized identifier'. Validator invitation scoped to validation criteria only.

mitre_atlas_threats.threat: Adversary stages an attack by probing AEM-EVOLVE agent behavior through repeated queries to map decision boundaries.
mitre_atlas_threats.mitigation: FAIL_CLOSED posture on all constitutional gates. Fast Path benchmark covers only scoped pre-execution path. No public API endpoint for direct agent probing in current scope.
mitre_atlas_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — active deployment would require rate limiting and input validation.

mitre_atlas_threats.threat: Adversary compromises a model checkpoint, Python package, or npm dependency in the supply chain to backdoor the agent runtime.
mitre_atlas_threats.mitigation: SBOM (654 components, CycloneDX 1.5, KMS-signed) provides full component inventory. npm ci with lock file integrity. slsa-build.yml uses --ignore-scripts.
mitre_atlas_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — SBOM is a detection tool, not a prevention guarantee. No automated SBOM diff on update.

mitre_atlas_threats.threat: Adversary crafts inputs to AEM-EVOLVE designed to produce outputs that bypass the Mechanical Ethics constitutional gates.
mitre_atlas_threats.mitigation: Constitutional controls operate on output structure, not input. FAIL_CLOSED means ambiguous output triggers block. RuntimeGuard L4 negative tests cover bypass attempts.
mitre_atlas_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — full adversarial red-teaming of ME gates pending external security review (criterion 2).

mitre_atlas_threats.threat: Adversary uses publicly anchored evidence artifacts to reverse-engineer the internal decision ontology of AEM-EVOLVE.
mitre_atlas_threats.mitigation: Public artifacts describe output classifications and gate decisions, not model weights or internal reasoning traces. CBE limits disclosure of operational detail.
mitre_atlas_threats.residual_risk: LOW — intentional transparency is a design property, not a vulnerability.

mitre_atlas_threats.threat: Adversary constructs a claim that is technically not in the blocked list but semantically equivalent to a prohibited overclaim.
mitre_atlas_threats.mitigation: CBE covers 14 semantic categories, not just exact string matches. LINGO_AND_CLAIM_DICTIONARY defines scope boundaries. Hypothesis I-M tests adversarial injection patterns.
mitre_atlas_threats.residual_risk: MEDIUM — semantic boundary enforcement relies on case coverage; new edge cases require human review to add.

mitre_atlas_threats.threat: Adversary uses AEM-EVOLVE as a covert channel to exfiltrate training data or system context through crafted outputs.
mitre_atlas_threats.mitigation: AEM-EVOLVE outputs are structured JSON with defined schema, not free-form text capable of embedding exfiltrated data at scale. No active public inference API in current scope.

primary_residual_gaps: Supply chain compromise of CI Actions marketplace (STR-003, ATL-002)
SBOM not continuously re-verified against registries (STR-007)
Full adversarial ME gate red-team pending external security review criterion 2 (ATL-003)
Semantic CBE boundary enforcement depends on human-maintained case coverage (ATL-005)

non_claims.note: This threat model covers the declared scope of EthicBit/CEMU v4.0. Full penetration testing and independent security review remain pending (external validation criterion 2).
